creator: gwright@antiope.com
text:
I deleted all of packages in the jail in which I run gitlab-ce.  I reinstalled everything using `pkg install www/gitlab-ce`, to get the new 15.0.2 release.

On restarting gitlab at the end of the procedure, sidekiq still fails:
```
root@gitlab:~ # service gitlab restart
Don't run Bundler as root. Bundler can ask for sudo if it is needed, and
installing your bundle as root will break this application for all non-root
users on this machine.
Don't run Bundler as root. Bundler can ask for sudo if it is needed, and
installing your bundle as root will break this application for all non-root
users on this machine.
Removing stale Sidekiq job dispatcher pid. This is most likely caused by Sidekiq crashing the last time it ran.
Shutting down GitLab web server
58555
Shutting down GitLab Workhorse
Shutting down Gitaly
...........
GitLab is not running.
Starting GitLab web server (puma)
Starting GitLab Sidekiq
Starting GitLab Workhorse
Starting Gitaly
.No pidfile found at /usr/local/www/gitlab-ce/tmp/pids/sidekiq-cluster.pid; is Sidekiq running?
.{"timestamp":"2022-06-15T17:14:58.301Z","pid":43164,"message":"Puma starting in cluster mode..."}
{"timestamp":"2022-06-15T17:14:58.301Z","pid":43164,"message":"* Puma version: 5.6.4 (ruby 3.0.4-p208) (\"Birdie's Version\")"}
{"timestamp":"2022-06-15T17:14:58.301Z","pid":43164,"message":"*  Min threads: 1"}
{"timestamp":"2022-06-15T17:14:58.301Z","pid":43164,"message":"*  Max threads: 16"}
{"timestamp":"2022-06-15T17:14:58.301Z","pid":43164,"message":"*  Environment: production"}
{"timestamp":"2022-06-15T17:14:58.301Z","pid":43164,"message":"*   Master PID: 43164"}
{"timestamp":"2022-06-15T17:14:58.301Z","pid":43164,"message":"*      Workers: 8"}
{"timestamp":"2022-06-15T17:14:58.301Z","pid":43164,"message":"*     Restarts: (✔) hot (✖) phased"}
{"timestamp":"2022-06-15T17:14:58.301Z","pid":43164,"message":"* Preloading application"}
...............................................{"timestamp":"2022-06-15T17:15:47.114Z","pid":43164,"message":"* Listening on unix:///usr/local/www/gitlab-ce/tmp/sockets/gitlab.socket"}
{"timestamp":"2022-06-15T17:15:47.114Z","pid":43164,"message":"! WARNING: Detected 1 Thread(s) started in app boot:"}
{"timestamp":"2022-06-15T17:15:47.115Z","pid":43164,"message":"! #\u003cThread:0x000000080d5a1f08 /usr/local/lib/ruby/gems/3.0/gems/rack-timeout-0.5.2/lib/rack/timeout/support/scheduler.rb:73 sleep\u003e - /usr/local/lib/ruby/gems/3.0/gems/rack-timeout-0.5.2/lib/rack/timeout/support/scheduler.rb:91:in `sleep'"}
{"timestamp":"2022-06-15T17:15:47.115Z","pid":43164,"message":"Use Ctrl-C to stop"}

Started in 49s.
The GitLab web server with pid 43164 is running.
The GitLab Sidekiq job dispatcher with pid 43174 is running.
The GitLab Workhorse with pid 43179 is running.
Gitaly with pid 43180 is running.
GitLab and all its components are up and running.
root@gitlab:~ # service gitlab status
The GitLab web server with pid 43164 is running.
The GitLab Sidekiq job dispatcher is not running.
The GitLab Workhorse with pid 43179 is running.
Gitaly with pid 43180 is running.
root@gitlab:~ #
```

I am running FreeBSD 13.1-RELEASE-p0.

I am starting to suspect that there is a stale library somewhere to which either redis or sidekiq links.  I will look into this.
